scope: Propagation and migration state, including the drift definition. Belongs here: the per-repo migrated/drifted state; spec propagation; the drift definition itself. Does not belong here: the SPEC.json model shape (→ SCHM). Boundary case: the drift definition lands in PROP, not SCHM — it is the propagation contract, and the SQL view computing it is merely enforcement.

requirements.statement: A rule change requires every bound site to re-prove green (GATE-02 applied across all tagged sites), so a partial update (cart fixed, invoice not) fails the gate.
requirements.why: Propagation has teeth — a rule change is not done until every bound site re-proves green; a partial update fails.

requirements.statement: A repo is drifted only when it is pinned to a spec version older than the version in which a requirement it actually references changed; behind-but-unaffected is not drift.
requirements.why: Drift must be precise or the gate cries wolf.

requirements.statement: spec propagation shows, for a superseded requirement, which repos migrated to the successor and which are still pinned behind.
requirements.why: The value moment — BILLING-009 shipped: api migrated, mobile still on BILLING-001.
